narrative: Dear Maria, imagine the admissions team looking at your application like they're piecing together the story of your journey to the bar exam, drawing from patterns they've seen in thousands of students just like you. Your Year 3 grades placed you in the bottom decile of your class—that stood out as the strongest signal, whispering that the rigors of upper-level law courses might foreshadow similar struggles on the bar. Right behind that was your LSAT score of 31, which, in the model's eyes, hinted at challenges tackling the intense reasoning demands ahead. Your Year 1 decile of 1 only reinforced this early pattern, painting a picture of consistent hurdles from the start. Even with your upper-middle family income, which the model saw as a modest tailwind but not enough to overcome the rest, it all started to tilt the scales. Your Hispanic background factored in as another pull toward the tougher path, based on broader trends the model has learned. Your undergraduate GPA of 2.8 added to that narrative, suggesting the academic grind might continue to be an uphill battle. Going full-time is a bold choice, but it nudged things slightly toward risk in this story. Your gender actually shone as a positive light, the one element pulling gently toward success amid the rest. Together, these threads wove a tale where the model saw a 62% chance of not passing the bar, leading to this tough call on your application. It's not the end of your story, though—many have rewritten theirs by targeting those early grades or test prep. You've got the determination; now it's about building on what you can control. Keep pushing forward.
